_fixture_note: SYNTHETIC fixture — NOT a real Slack API response. Hand-built to exercise time-gap context segmentation (see ISSUES.md, count-only context bug). It reproduces the shape that broke a capture: an older merge-session burst (five messages ~25 min apart), a ~5-day (~125h) silence, then a recent PR-comment anchor burst (two messages minutes before the reacted message, then the reacted message itself). This is the CHRONOLOGICAL order (anchor last) that get_context produces after messages.reverse() — feed it straight into _segment_context_by_gap. All users, timestamps, PR references, and text are fabricated; no real workspace, DM, person, or ticket is represented. To exercise the O1 test against a real seam-fetched window instead, drop a file named context-window.real.json alongside this one (that name is gitignored so a real transcript never lands in git); the O1 test prefers the .real.json when present, else falls back to this synthetic file.
